---
title: "Fix Stata path errors"
subtitle: "BNR info-hub Technical Manual"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
---

# Fix Stata path errors


## Purpose

Use this guide when a Stata DO file cannot find a path, dataset, ADO file, output folder, or local configuration file.

The usual causes are:

- Stata was started from the wrong working directory
- `bnr_paths_LOCAL.do` is missing or has the wrong paths
- the private data folder is missing
- the expected frozen dataset is not in the expected location
- an ADO helper is not in `scripts/stata/ado/`

# Stage 1: Confirm the working directory

:::{.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Step 1. Check where Stata is running

In Stata, run:

```stata
pwd
```

The result should be the repo root:

```text
C:/yoshimi-hot/output/analyse-bnr/info-hub
```

If not, set it explicitly:

```stata
cd "C:/yoshimi-hot/output/analyse-bnr/info-hub"
```
:::

# Stage 2: Check local path configuration

:::{.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Step 1. Confirm the local path file exists

From the repo root in Stata, run:

```stata
confirm file "scripts/stata/config/bnr_paths_LOCAL.do"
```

If this fails, copy:

```text
scripts/stata/config/bnr_paths_TEMPLATE.do
```

and rename the copy to:

```text
scripts/stata/config/bnr_paths_LOCAL.do
```
:::

:::{.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Step 2. Check the first two globals

Open:

```text
scripts/stata/config/bnr_paths_LOCAL.do
```

Check these lines:

```stata
global BNR_REPO     "C:/yoshimi-hot/output/analyse-bnr/info-hub"
global BNR_PRIVATE  "C:/yoshimi-hot/output/analyse-bnr/info-hub-private"
```

Both paths must match the local machine.
:::

:::{.callout-warning collapse="true"}
## Do not commit local paths

`bnr_paths_LOCAL.do` is machine-specific and should not be committed to Git.

Only the template should be committed:

```text
scripts/stata/config/bnr_paths_TEMPLATE.do
```
:::

# Stage 3: Check the private data folder

:::{.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Step 1. Confirm the private folder exists

In PowerShell, run:

```powershell
Test-Path C:\yoshimi-hot\output\analyse-bnr\info-hub-private
```

Expected result:

```text
True
```
:::

:::{.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Step 2. Confirm the frozen dataset location

The 2023 CVD prep file expects the frozen source dataset under:

```text
info-hub-private/data/frozen/releases/y2023/m12/
```

For the case-count pilot, the expected dataset is:

```text
bnr-cvd-indiv-full-202312-v01.dta
```
:::

# Stage 4: Check ADO helpers

:::{.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Step 1. Confirm active ADO files

Active ADO helpers should live here:

```text
scripts/stata/ado/
```

In Stata, after loading local paths, run:

```stata
adopath
```

Check that the project ADO folder appears.
:::

# Stage 5: Rerun the failing command

After fixing a path issue, rerun the same command that failed, for example:

```stata
do "scripts/stata/briefings/cvd_cases_2023/cvd_cases_2023.do"
```
